dataset: USLCI FY24 Q4 (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database)

pi_items.source: Task description explicitly requires: 'Define a global or local parameter named substitution_ratio'
Task description states: 'with a value of 0.9' and negative input formula '-1 * substitution_ratio' = '-0.9'
Confirmed in task.json description, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh scripts

pi_items.verified_value: Polyethylene, high density, HDPE, virgin resin, at plant
pi_items.source: Task description specifies 'Polyethylene, high density, resin, at plant'. Web search confirmed this flow exists in USLCI database and Global LCA Data Access (GLAD) database. Verified flow naming convention from NREL LCA Commons.

ISO 14040/14044 standard LCA methodology. Web search confirmed 'system expansion' as official ISO terminology for the substitution approach used in LCA for handling co-products and avoided burdens. Task description states: 'Model the environmental benefit of recycling using System Expansion (substitution)'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly available LCA data: USLCI FY24 Q4 database (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory from NREL/FLCAC) and TRACI 2.1 impact assessment methods from EPA (Figshare item 25782786). The task requires creating an OpenLCA process for HDPE recycling modeling system expansion with a negative input of -0.9 kg virgin polyethylene to represent avoided burden. The exact flow 'Polyethylene, high density, HDPE, virgin resin, at plant' exists in the USLCI database. Global Warming is a standard impact category in TRACI 2.1 (100-year global warming potential measured in kg CO2-eq). System expansion is the ISO 14040/14044 standard methodology for handling multifunctionality in LCA through avoided burden allocation.
